steps.title: Set up the proportion
steps.content: The ratio $12$ to $2$ is equivalent to the ratio $60$ to $2k$, so $$\frac{12}{2}=\frac{60}{2k}.$$ The left side simplifies to $6$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: $$6=\frac{60}{2k}$$ Multiply both sides by $2k$: $$12k=60.$$ Divide by $12$: $$k=5.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $0.4$: Then $2k=0.8$, so the ratio $60$ to $2k$ is $60$ to $0.8$, not $6$. ✗
  • Choice B — $5$: Then $2k=10$, so $\frac{60}{10}=6$, matching $\frac{12}{2}=6$. ✓
  • Choice C — $10$: Then $2k=20$, so the ratio is $60$ to $20$, or $3$. ✗
  • Choice D — $120$: Then $2k=240$, so the ratio is $60$ to $240$, or $0.25$. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
